description_long: This is a realistic sunflower with a tall, slender green stem supporting a large, round flower head. The flower head features a central disc of densely packed, yellowish-brown florets surrounded by a single whorl of bright yellow petals that radiate outward. The petals are slightly pointed and overlap each other, creating a full, symmetrical appearance. Two green leaves, one larger and one smaller, are attached to the stem at different heights, both with a broad, ovate shape and visible veins. The back of the flower head is covered by green, pointed sepals that cradle the petals. The overall structure is typical of a Helianthus annuus, commonly found in gardens, parks, and urban green spaces.
description: A tall sunflower with a green stem, broad ovate leaves, and a large yellow flower head with overlapping petals and a central disc.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin green stem, one ovate leaf, and the profile of the yellow flower head with visible sepals.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full, round face of the sunflower with a central disc and radiating yellow petals.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the stem, a single leaf, and the edge of the flower head with overlapping petals.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the green sepals covering the back of the flower head and both leaves attached to the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a single, radial whorl around the central disc, overlapping slightly and forming a symmetrical, daisy-like pattern typical of sunflowers.
